name: Miniforge Installer
description: Install Miniforge in the development container.

version.description:
Conda version to install (e.g. '26.3.1').
Accepts a conda version string, not a Miniforge release tag.
Defaults to 'latest', which resolves to the current latest release via the GitHub API.
For a specific version, the script finds the highest Miniforge build number for that conda version
(e.g. '24.7.1' resolves to tag '24.7.1-2').
If the already-installed conda version matches the resolved version, the install is
skipped silently regardless of the 'if_exists' setting.

if_exists.description:
What to do when conda is already installed at prefix.
'skip' — warn and continue to post-install steps (idempotent).
'fail' — print an error and exit non-zero.
'reinstall' — uninstall the existing installation then install fresh.
'update' — update the base conda environment to the resolved version.

preserve_envs.description:
When if_exists is 'reinstall', export all non-base conda environments before
uninstalling and recreate them after reinstalling.
Each environment is exported with --from-history; on recreation failure the YAML
is kept at /tmp/conda-env-preserve for manual recovery.
Defaults to true.

preserve_config.description:
When if_exists is 'reinstall', skip conda init --reverse and preserve .condarc
and .conda directories during uninstall.
Set to false to perform a full clean uninstall.
Defaults to true.

update_base.description:
Update the base conda environment.
This is done by running `conda update --all`.
This is not recommended for production environments.

prefix.description:
Path to the conda installation directory (installation root/prefix).
'auto' (default) — resolves to /opt/conda when running as root (the de-facto standard used by continuumio Docker images and the devcontainer ecosystem), or ~/miniforge3 when running as non-root (the Miniforge installer's own default prefix).
An explicit path — used as-is; the directory is created if it does not exist.
With symlink=true, a symlink is created from the canonical prefix to $PREFIX when they differ: /opt/conda -> $PREFIX for root, $HOME/miniforge3 -> $PREFIX for non-root.

export_path.description:
Controls which shell startup files receive the PATH export for $PREFIX/bin.
'auto' writes to all relevant system-wide files (Case A: public install + root)
or user-scoped files (Case B: user install or non-root).
'' (empty) skips all PATH writes.
Newline-separated list of absolute paths: writes only to those files.

symlink.description:
Create a directory symlink from the canonical conda prefix to $prefix when prefix resolves to a non-default path.
The symlink location is determined by whether $prefix falls under any user's home directory (as listed in /etc/passwd):
  System-wide (prefix is not under any user home): creates /opt/conda -> $prefix.
  User-scoped  (prefix is under a user home):        creates $HOME/miniforge3 -> $prefix.
Ensures the containerEnv PATH entries always resolve to the conda binaries regardless of the chosen prefix.
No-op when $prefix is already the chosen target path.

shell_activations.description:
Space-separated list of shell names to write conda initialization blocks for.
For each listed shell, writes an idempotent conda init block to the appropriate
system-wide (root) or user-scoped (non-root) rc file.
Supported: 'bash', 'zsh'. Set to '' to skip all activation writes.
Examples: 'bash', 'zsh', 'bash zsh'.

activate_env.description:
Conda environment to activate at shell startup.
'base' sets auto_activate_base=true in ~/.condarc (the conda-native mechanism).
Any other name appends a 'conda activate <env>' line to the rc files.
'' (empty) skips activation setup entirely.
Only takes effect when shell_activations is non-empty.

write_group.description:
OS group for shared write access to the conda prefix.
Non-empty — create this group (if absent), add all resolved users to it, and apply group-write bits on the prefix so group members can install packages.
'' (empty) — skip group setup; only the install owner has write access.

add_current_user.description:
Include the current user (the user running the installer, or SUDO_USER if set) in the resolved user list for per-user config writes and write-permission group membership.
Root is deferred: only included as a fallback when no other non-root user is resolved.

add_remote_user.description:
Include the devcontainer remoteUser (from the _REMOTE_USER env var) in the resolved user list for per-user config writes and write-permission group membership.
Ignored when _REMOTE_USER is unset or empty. Root is excluded from this path.

add_container_user.description:
Include the devcontainer containerUser (from the _CONTAINER_USER env var) in the resolved user list for per-user config writes and write-permission group membership.
Ignored when _CONTAINER_USER is unset or empty. Root is excluded from this path.

add_users.description:
Comma-separated list of additional usernames to include in the resolved user list for per-user config writes and write-permission group membership.
Root is accepted here (unlike the auto-detected paths above).

interactive.description:
Run the installer in interactive mode.
This allows the installer to prompt the user.
The default is to run the installer in non-interactive mode.

installer_dir.description:
Path to a directory to download the installer to.
This is the directory where the Miniforge installer will be downloaded.

keep_installer.description: Keep the Miniforge installer script and checksum after installation instead of removing them.

logfile.description: Log all output (stdout + stderr) to this file in addition to console.
